key_insight: Adding a controlled amount of Al to AT40 plasma-sprayed ceramic coatings densifies the microstructure and boosts toughness without sacrificing hardness, with 10% Al giving the best overall balance.
engineering_relevance: The study shows that composition and spray-parameter tuning can turn brittle ceramic coatings into tougher, more durable metal–ceramic layers for wear- and impact-resistant surfaces.
method: Atmospheric plasma spraying of AT40/Al composite coatings with 10%, 20%, and 30% Al
Parameter study on arc power, spray distance, and gun traverse speed
SEM, XRD, EDS, and grey relational analysis for structure–property evaluation
evidence_signal: At 10% Al, porosity dropped from 8.5% to 3.2%, and the optimized coating reached 519.68 HV with a 45.3% impact-resistance improvement over pure AT40.
watch_item: The performance gains depend on tightly controlled spraying conditions and Al content, so scaling to different equipment or substrates may require re-optimization.
paper_title: Preparation and Mechanism Study of Plasma-Sprayed Metal–Ceramic Composite Coatings Based on Microstructure
